intro: Psychotria ombrophila, also known as Ipenema or Green Tea of the Forest, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria ombrophila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Psychotria ombrophila is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the form of capsules or tinctures, and is often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
